name: The Best Vegetarian Chili in the World
instructions: 1. Heat the olive oil in a large, heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven over medium heat until shimmering. 2. Add the diced onion and season immediately with the dried oregano, salt, ground cumin, and bay leaves. Stir well to coat the onion with the spices. Cook, stirring frequently, for about 3-5 minutes until the onion becomes soft, translucent, and fragrant. The spices will become aromatic. 3. Add the diced celery, bell peppers, jalapeños, and minced garlic to the pot. Stir everything together and continue cooking for another 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until all the vegetables are softened and the garlic is fragrant. Don't let the garlic brown. 4. Stir in the green chile peppers and cook for another 2-3 minutes until they're heated through and well incorporated with the other vegetables. 5. Add the tofu (note: use tofu as vegetarian burger crumbles substitute), breaking it up with a wooden spoon into crumbles. Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot with a lid, and let it simmer gently for about 5 minutes to allow the tofu to absorb the flavors. 6. Stir in the crushed canned tomatoes, chili powder, and black pepper. Mix everything together thoroughly, making sure the chili powder is evenly distributed throughout the mixture. 7. Add all three types of beans: kidney beans, chickpeas (garbanzo beans), and black beans. Stir well to combine everything. The chili should be thick and hearty. 8. Bring the mixture to a boil over medium-high heat, then immediately reduce the heat to low. Cover and let the chili simmer gently for about 45 minutes, stirring occasionally. This allows all the flavors to meld together beautifully. If the chili becomes too thick, add a splash of water or vegetable broth. 9. After 45 minutes, stir in the canned corn and cook for an additional 5 minutes until the corn is heated through. Taste and adjust seasoning with more salt, pepper, or chili powder if needed. Remove the bay leaves before serving. 10. Ladle the hot chili into bowls and serve with your favorite toppings such as shredded cheese, sliced jalapeños, diced avocados, sour cream, chopped cilantro, or tortilla chips. This chili tastes even better the next day as the flavors continue to develop!
simpleInstructions: 1. Heat olive oil in large pot over medium heat. 2. Add onion with oregano, salt, cumin, and bay leaves; cook 3-5 minutes until soft. 3. Add celery, bell peppers, jalapeños, and garlic; cook 5-7 minutes. 4. Stir in green chiles and cook 2-3 minutes. 5. Add tofu, reduce heat, cover, and simmer 5 minutes. 6. Stir in crushed tomatoes, chili powder, and black pepper. 7. Add kidney beans, chickpeas, and black beans; stir well. 8. Bring to boil, reduce heat, cover, and simmer 45 minutes. 9. Stir in corn and cook 5 more minutes. Remove bay leaves. 10. Serve hot with toppings like cheese, jalapeños, avocado, or sour cream.
